title: Compiling Temporal Numeric Planning into Discrete PDDL+: Extended Version

The sequence of states ¯s0 . . . , ¯s¯m constructed in this way Conclusion
satisfies all the PDDL+ semantic constraints for ¯Π. In this paper we presented a compilation from PDDL 2.1
level 3 into PDDL+, proving its soundness and completeness. It was commonly known that PDDL+ could express
Experimental Evaluation durative actions, but in thi spaper we provide the first formal
We experimented with our compilation over a selection of account of this fact, providing mechanisms to deal with the
temporal numeric domains. We focused our attention on subtleties of the PDDL semantics. In particular, we provide a
problems requiring intertwined reasoning between numeric mechanism to faithfully impose durative conditions and the "no-moving-target rule" presented in the PDDL 2.1 paper Micheli, A.; and Scala, E. 2019. Temporal Planning with
(Fox and Long 2003). Our compilation is not only of theo- Temporal Metric Trajectory Constraints. In The Thirty-Third
retical interest, but it is also shown to be useful on complex AAAI Conference on Artificial Intelligence, AAAI 2019,
temporal numeric planning problems. 7675–7682. As future work, we would like to extend the compilation Percassi, F.; Scala, E.; and Vallati, M. 2025. On the Notion
to PDDL 2.1. level 4, hence including continuous change. of Plan Quality for PDDL+.
